title: SoFi Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: SoFi Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Heading to SoFi Stadium for a Rams game, a concert, or the FIFA World Cup 2026 means knowing exactly what’s allowed through the gates.
The stadium’s clear bag policy stops most people at security, and if your bag doesn’t comply, you’ll either walk back to your car or pay $20 to check it at a valet.
In this guide, you’ll learn what clear bags are permitted, which small clutches work, how to measure your bag correctly, what items are absolutely prohibited, and your options if you arrive with a non-compliant bag.
Quick Reference: Bag Policy Compliance Chart
We’ve researched the official SoFi Stadium rules and the NFL clear bag policy to give you everything you need to pack right and get inside without delays.
|Bag Type||Size Limit||Material||Status||Notes|
|Clear backpack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Transparency required|
|Clear fanny pack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||One-gallon freezer bag alternative|
|Small clutch||4.5″ × 6.5″||Any (opaque OK)||✅ Allowed||Wallet-sized, plus one clear bag|
|Clear diaper bag||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Subject to inspection|
|Standard backpack||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Even if under limit|
|Opaque handbag||Over 4.5″ × 6.5″||Any||❌ Prohibited||Must use valet or leave behind|
|Rolling luggage||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Use bag storage nearby|
|Briefcase||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Transfer items to clear bag|
SoFi Stadium Clear Bag Policy
SoFi Stadium requires transparent bags so security can see contents without opening them. Permitted clear bags must be plastic, vinyl, or PVC and cannot exceed 12 inches tall by 6 inches wide by 12 inches deep.
This core rule applies to 95% of guests and is non-negotiable.
Bags must be completely see-through with no tint, hologram, patterns, or stickers. Canvas with clear panels doesn’t qualify—the entire bag must be transparent.
Clear Plastic, Vinyl, and PVC Materials
All three approved materials work equally at SoFi. Clear plastic is rigid (makeup organizer style). Vinyl is flexible and weather-resistant.
PVC is durable polyvinyl chloride. Most stadium-goers prefer vinyl or PVC because they fold flat and resist damage.
Avoid canvas, waterproof nylon, or laminated materials. Material must be 100% transparent for instant security inspection.
One-Gallon Clear Freezer Bags
One-gallon clear plastic freezer bags (Ziploc style) are pre-approved. A gallon bag measures roughly 11 by 10.5 inches, fitting under the size limit. Disposable, lightweight, and under $5 per box, they work well for one-time events.
The bag must be completely clear with a functional seal. Test at home to confirm it meets the 12x6x12 standard.
Bag Size Limit: 12″ x 6″ x 12″
This is the most important number: 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches. If any dimension exceeds this by even an inch, you cannot bring it inside. Security measures bags at the gate with zero flexibility.
Maximum Dimensions Explained
The 12-inch height accommodates small backpacks and tote bags. The 6-inch width prevents unwieldy bags in crowds.
The 12-inch depth blocks bulky duffel bags. These limits balance security efficiency with practicality—oversized bags create entry choke points and obstruct sightlines.
How to Measure Your Bag
- Height — Stand your bag upright and measure bottom to top, including handles. Round up to the nearest half-inch.
- Width — Measure left to right at the widest point (usually the front opening). This is typically the smallest dimension.
- Depth — Measure front to back. A small-looking bag often measures deeper than expected.
Use your phone’s measurement app or physical ruler. A five-minute confirmation beats walking back to your car.
SoFi Stadium Policy: Prohibited Items
Security enforces the prohibited list strictly. Violating it means denied entry with no exceptions. The list includes all bags larger than allowed plus specific safety risks.
Backpacks, Coolers, and Luggage
Traditional backpacks are prohibited even if clear and undersized. A typical hiking backpack measures 16x10x8 inches minimum, exceeding the width and depth limits.
Coolers of any kind (hard or soft-sided) are banned. Luggage, duffel bags, and suitcases are completely prohibited. Store airport luggage at nearby facilities; SoFi doesn’t hold items overnight.
Non-Clear Purses and Briefcases
Non-clear purses larger than the 4.5×6.5-inch exception are prohibited. A typical handbag (8x10x4 inches) won’t be allowed.
Briefcases, work bags, fanny packs, crossbody bags, cinch bags, and camera bags over the size limit are all banned. Transfer documents to a compliant clutch or clear bag instead.
Bag Exceptions and Allowances
The clear bag rule has exactly three exceptions, and each requires specific conditions. Understanding when exceptions apply saves time and prevents arguments at security.
Small Clutches and Fanny Packs (4.5″ x 6.5″)
The single biggest exception to the clear bag rule is small non-clear clutches and fanny packs measuring no more than 4.5 inches by 6.5 inches. This is your loophole for a real purse or wallet.
A 4.5×6.5-inch clutch holds a phone, keys, credit cards, and ID with minimal extra space. Most designer crossbody bags and formal clutches fit comfortably within these dimensions. The key detail: measurements include the handle and strap, not just the bag body.
You can bring one small clutch in addition to your clear bag. This means one clear 12x6x12 bag plus one non-clear clutch up to 4.5×6.5. You cannot bring two clear bags or two clutches.
Fanny packs and hip bags under 4.5×6.5 inches are permitted. Many fans prefer small fanny packs over clutches because they’re hands-free and fit snugly at the waist. The same size restriction applies.
Medically Necessary Bags and Diaper Bags
Medically necessary items are exempt from the clear bag policy after additional screening. This includes insulin pumps, EpiPen carriers, hearing aid cases, mobility devices, and other medical equipment.
Notify security staff in advance if possible. Expect to enter through a designated accessibility gate and allow extra time for inspection.
Diaper bags are permitted under two conditions: they must be clear and meet the 12x6x12 size limit. Opaque diaper bags of any size are prohibited.
The “necessary for infants” exception does not override the bag material or size requirements. If you’re traveling with an infant and a non-compliant diaper bag, use a clear bag instead or plan to use the bag valet service.
Parents with medically complex children sometimes require larger bags for medications and supplies.
Contact SoFi Stadium Guest Services before your visit to arrange accommodations. Documentation may be required.
Extra Security Screening Process
When you bring an exception item like a medical bag or small clutch, security opens and inspects it completely. Expect a thorough check of every pocket and compartment.
This adds 3-5 minutes to entry time, so arrive early if you’re using an exception.
Items inside must comply with prohibited item rules as well. You can’t circumvent the clear bag policy by hiding prohibited items inside an allowed clutch. Security scans the interior.
Bag Valet is located at the NE, NW, SW, and SE corners of SoFi Stadium’s exterior perimeter. The service charges $20 per bag from check-in until shortly after the event concludes. SoFi Cardholders (the stadium’s membership program) check bags free.
Crossbody Bags: What’s Allowed?
Crossbody bags create constant confusion because their appeal (wearable, hands-free, practical) clashes with the size rules. The key distinction: it’s the size that matters, not the style.
What Qualifies as a Crossbody Bag?
A crossbody bag is any bag worn across the chest or shoulder, hanging at the hip. The style includes messenger bags, sling bags, crossbody purses, and hip slings. The style itself is not the determining factor for entry.
What determines entry is whether the bag meets the clear requirement and size limit. A clear crossbody bag measuring 10x5x8 inches is permitted. A non-clear crossbody bag measuring 8x6x6 inches is prohibited unless it’s the specifically approved 4.5×6.5-inch clutch style.
The crossbody strap doesn’t change the measurement. Measure the bag body itself, excluding the strap length. If the body exceeds 12x6x12, it’s prohibited regardless of how you wear it.
Size and Material Requirements
Your crossbody bag must satisfy two conditions simultaneously: the material must be clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC, and the dimensions must not exceed 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches. Violating either requirement means denied entry.
Many popular crossbody bags in stores measure 9x7x3 inches or similar, fitting within the limit. However, most designer crossbody bags exceed the width or depth limit. Check the product specs before buying or bringing one to the stadium.
Clear crossbody bags are less stylish than traditional options, so many people wonder about alternatives. The answer is simple: bring a small non-clear clutch (4.5×6.5) instead of a crossbody, or switch to a clear bag for stadium events.
SoFi Stadium Water Bottle Policy
While technically separate from the bag policy, water bottle rules directly impact what you can carry inside, so knowing them prevents confusion at entry gates.
Permitted Bottle Types and Sizes
You may bring one factory-sealed 20-ounce water bottle into SoFi Stadium. The bottle must be sealed (unopened) before entry.
Common examples include Aquafina, Dasani, or store-brand bottled water. Once you enter, you cannot bring opened bottles back outside security and bring them back in.
Check the Chargers or Rams official websites for event-specific water bottle policies, as some special events may have different rules.
Empty, reusable water bottles under 20 ounces are also permitted. This includes metal, plastic, or fabric bottles as long as they’re empty at entry.
SoFi has free water refill stations on multiple concourse levels, so bringing an empty reusable bottle is economical for long events.
Hard-sided water bottles, collapsible bottles, and bottles larger than 20 ounces are prohibited. Vacuum-insulated bottles (Hydro Flask style) are typically allowed if they’re empty and under the size limit, but confirm at your specific gate since policies vary slightly.
Empty vs. Full Bottles
An unopened, sealed 20-ounce factory bottle counts as one item and fits inside your clear bag. An empty reusable bottle takes up more space in your bag but costs nothing to refill at the stadium.
You cannot bring open or partially filled water bottles. Bringing an open bottle of water and intending to refill it at the fountain is not permitted—you must enter with it empty. This rule exists for security screening purposes.
FAQs
Can I bring a belt bag or small crossbody to SoFi Stadium?
Only if it’s clear and measures 12x6x12 inches or smaller. Non-clear belt bags must be the specific 4.5×6.5-inch clutch size. Most fashion belt bags exceed these dimensions, so measure before arriving.
What about clear backpacks for school or hiking?
Clear backpacks over 12x6x12 inches are prohibited. Measuring matters more than the style. Many clear backpacks marketed as stadium-approved actually measure 15x10x8 inches and will be rejected at SoFi gates. Check the manufacturer’s specifications carefully before purchase.
Do I need to bring a bag at all?
No. SoFi actively encourages guests to leave bags at home if possible. Arriving without a bag speeds up entry significantly and reduces security wait times. Bring only what fits in your pockets if that’s an option.
What happens if my bag is slightly over the size limit?
Security will deny entry. There is no flexibility. A 12.5-inch-tall clear bag will be turned away. If you arrive with an oversized bag, you have three options: return to your vehicle, use the bag valet for $20, or check with Guest Services about exception eligibility.
Can I bring a Lululemon belt bag or similar branded small bags?
Branded small bags must still meet the clear bag requirement or the 4.5×6.5-inch clutch exception. Most designer belt bags exceed the clutch size limit and aren’t clear, so they’re prohibited. Always measure and check the material, not the brand name.
Is the clear bag policy the same for concerts as NFL games?
Yes, the policy applies uniformly to all events. Sports, concerts, conventions, and other gatherings at SoFi use the identical clear bag and size rules. The NFL enforces this policy across all stadiums, ensuring consistency.
What if I have a medical bag larger than 12x6x12 inches?
Contact SoFi Stadium Guest Services before your visit at 1-844-SOFI-888 to arrange accommodations. Medical necessity may qualify for an exception with proper documentation. Waiting until gate entry to explain won’t get you through.
Can I bring a purse with a small clear window to show my ID and cards?
No. The entire bag (all panels and sides) must be transparent. A purse with a single clear window is not a clear bag under stadium rules.
Does the bag have to be new or unused?
No, the bag just needs to meet the material and size specifications. A used clear vinyl bag that you’ve brought to other stadiums is fine as long as it’s still transparent and within limits.
What about the difference between “clutch” and “small bag”?
In stadium policy, these terms are used interchangeably. A small clutch is any bag under 4.5×6.5 inches, whether it’s traditional clutch style, a small crossbody, a small shoulder bag, or a wallet on a strap.
If I exceed the bag size limit by less than an inch, will security let me through?
Unlikely. Security uses measuring devices and applies rules consistently. A bag measuring 12.5 inches in any dimension will be rejected, even if the excess is small. Round up your measurements to be safe.
What’s the best clear bag to bring to SoFi Stadium?
Clear vinyl tote bags are the most versatile option. They’re durable, fold flat when empty, and remain transparent even after repeated use. One-gallon freezer bags are a budget-friendly alternative for occasional visits. Clear backpacks work if they measure under 12x6x12 inches, though this size is hard to find. For FIFA World Cup 2026 matches, expect slightly enhanced security protocols, so arriving with a pre-approved clear bag eliminates entry delays.

title: NRG Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: NRG Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Attending events at NRG Stadium requires planning what you bring. The clear bag policy limits what fits through security, and bags exceeding size restrictions get turned away at the gate with no storage options available.
Whether you’re catching a Houston Texans game, concert, or attending the FIFA World Cup 2026, understanding these rules saves time and frustration.
This guide covers exact bag size restrictions, which clear bags work, what happens with crossbody bags and water bottles, and medical accommodations.
Quick Reference: Bag Policy Compliance Chart
|What’s Allowed||Dimensions||Details|
|Clear bags||12″ × 6″ × 12″ max||Plastic, vinyl, or PVC material|
|One-gallon freezer bags||Standard freezer size||Cost under $5, disposable option|
|Small clutch purses||4.5″ × 6.5″ max||Can carry with one clear bag|
|Medical equipment||Varies||Subject to security inspection|
|Water bottles (empty)||Event-dependent||Check venue for refill stations|
|What’s Prohibited||Why||Alternatives|
|Backpacks (any size)||Security inspection issues||Use approved clear bags instead|
|Large purses/totes||Exceed size limits||Switch to small clutches|
|Coolers/briefcases||Prevent quick screening||Purchase beverages inside stadium|
|Sealed packages||Cannot be inspected||Bring only unsealed items|
|Luggage||Storage not available||Use storage services near venue|
NRG Stadium Clear Bag Policy Requirements
Clear bags form the foundation of NRG’s entry security. Only transparent bags made from clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC qualify. The requirement applies to all events, from regular season Texans games to major tournaments.
The Houston Texans officially adopted the NFL’s clear bag policy to speed up entry and enhance safety measures.
This standardized approach across all NFL stadiums creates consistency for fans attending multiple venues.
Approved clear bag options include standard gallon-sized freezer bags or purpose-built stadium bags marketed specifically for arena entry.
Approved Clear Bag Types
Clear bags must be transparent enough for security staff to see contents immediately. Standard one-gallon freezer bags with zip seals work perfectly and cost under $5 for a pack.
These disposable options mean you don’t need to invest in specialty bags for casual attendance.
Purpose-built clear stadium bags range from $15-50 and last for years. Major retailers like Amazon and sporting goods stores stock NRG-compliant options designed with comfortable straps and organizational pockets while maintaining transparency.
Mesh bags don’t qualify because they lack the clear plastic covering required for proper inspection. Similarly, bags with any non-transparent panels or tinted plastic get rejected.
One-Gallon Freezer Bags
A one-gallon freezer bag represents the most cost-effective option and appears frequently in stadium parking lots. These bags feature adequate space for phone, wallet, sunglasses, and light layers without approaching the size limit.
Freezer bags are specifically mentioned in the official NRG guidance as an approved option that works year-round. Simply bring your own or purchase them at convenience stores near the stadium.
Small Clutch Purses
In addition to clear options, small clutch purses offer an alternative if you prefer non-transparent bags. These must stay under 4.5″ x 6.5″ and include handles or straps. The clutch can accompany one clear bag, giving you flexibility for personal style.
Non-clear clutches work because their small dimensions prevent concealment of problematic items. They’re perfect for attendees who want to carry cards and ID in a traditional wallet without the clear bag.
Bag Size Restrictions
NRG enforces exact dimensions with no exceptions. Clear bags maxing out at 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches represent the ceiling for transparent options. Any measurements exceeding these limits gets denied, regardless of how minimal the overage.
Security staff verify dimensions quickly using standardized measurement guides. Bags that appear to fit but technically exceed limits still get rejected. According to NRG Park’s official guidance, compliance with specific measurements is mandatory across all event types.
Maximum Dimensions (12″ x 6″ x 12″)
These dimensions accommodate most daily essentials while maintaining security efficiency. A 12″ x 6″ x 12″ clear bag holds a smartphone, wallet, keys, small snacks, and a light jacket without reaching capacity.
Width of 6 inches prevents the bag from expanding significantly at the sides. Many clear stadium bags compress slightly when full, making the measurement effective without excessive restriction.
Small Clutch Size Limits
Clutch purses capping at 4.5″ x 6.5″ create intentional limitation that prevents problem items. This size standard applies whether your clutch includes a handle or strap.
The measurement references the bag’s main body, not including protruding handles or chains. A clutch with a 2-inch handle extending beyond the 4.5″ dimension still complies if the primary body meets specifications.
What Exceeds Size Restrictions?
Tote bags, large purses, backpacks of any size, laptop bags, and diaper bags fail the dimensional test even with open designs. Standard personal bags people use daily almost always exceed either the clear bag or clutch limits.
Weekend travel bags, even small ones designed for short trips, universally exceed the 12″ x 6″ x 12″ boundary. Many people arrive expecting to bring these and face disappointment when security turns them away.
Prohibited Items & Exceptions
NRG prohibits entire categories beyond just bag sizes. Weapons, outside food and beverages (except medical/child exceptions), recording equipment with professional lenses, and sealed packages that can’t be inspected all get denied.
The official banned items list includes firearms and ammunition, which Texas law specifically addresses through Penal Code §30.06 and §30.07 for specified locations. Explosive devices, fireworks, and noisemakers create obvious safety concerns.
Backpacks & Luggage
No backpacks qualify regardless of size. Even small hiking daypacks designed to minimize bulk exceed policy. Luggage pieces designed for air travel automatically fail. This applies to rolling luggage, soft-sided duffel bags, and cabin-sized options.
The blanket backpack prohibition stems from the inability to quickly visually inspect compartments and hidden sections during rapid security screening. Weekender bags treated as luggage also face rejection.
Coolers & Briefcases
Hard-sided coolers don’t qualify as approved bags. Soft coolers with insulation also fail because their dimensions typically exceed limits and they prevent efficient visual inspection. Briefcases and work bags designed for professional settings aren’t permitted.
Business travelers attending events need alternative strategies for transporting laptops or work materials. Most venues like NRG don’t accommodate these needs during public events.
Exceptions & Special Accommodations
Medically necessary items receive consideration after proper inspection. Prescription medications, insulin pumps, EpiPens, and medical devices for managing health conditions get approved on a case-by-case basis.
Contact NRG security in advance for unusual medical needs. Diaper bags and baby supplies for infants qualify for inspection and potential accommodation. Nursing mothers have access to dedicated rooms through guest services.
Are Crossbody Bags Allowed?
Crossbody bags only work if they meet the small clutch specifications of 4.5″ x 6.5″ or smaller. The key factor isn’t the strap or crossbody design—it’s the actual bag dimensions.
Many people assume crossbody design alone creates an exception. This misunderstanding causes repeated denials when attendees show up with standard crossbody purses that exceed the clutch size limit despite having adjustable straps.
Crossbody Bag Guidelines
A crossbody bag qualifies when its main body dimensions stay under the clutch limit. The strap length doesn’t matter. A small crossbody purse designed specifically for stadiums with an adjustable strap works perfectly.
Popular stadium crossbody options available online clearly state their compliance with venue policies. These typically measure around 4″ x 5″ with a strap that adjusts from shoulder length down to waist carry.
Strap & Handle Rules
NRG doesn’t restrict the strap material, length, or adjustability. A removable strap or crossbody configuration doesn’t exempt a bag from size requirements. The clutch simply needs to fit within the stated dimensions.
Chains, decorative handles, or leather straps don’t factor into the dimensional measurement if they’re separate from the main bag structure. What matters is the container itself.
Crossbody vs. Clutch Distinction
The terminology doesn’t determine approval. A small clutch worn on a shoulder strap is a crossbody clutch and complies fully. A large crossbody purse exceeds limits and gets denied despite its strap.
Think in terms of bag dimensions rather than style names. “Crossbody” describes how you wear it, while “clutch” describes its size category. NRG bases decisions on measurements, not terminology.
What About Water Bottles?
Water bottles face event-specific rules that vary by situation. For regular Texans games, outside beverages including water bottles must be sealed and unopened at entry, then remain sealed throughout the event.
The FIFA World Cup 2026 will implement heightened security protocols distinct from standard NFL games. Expect stricter beverage restrictions during the tournament, likely requiring bottles to be purchased at the venue.
Empty Bottles Policy
Empty water bottles designed for refilling face inconsistent treatment. Some events allow bringing empty bottles to fill at water stations. Others prohibit all bottles, even empty ones, to prevent potential misuse.
Check the specific event details before arriving. Texans games typically permit empty reusable bottles for refilling, but concerts or special events may differ.
Sealed vs. Open Containers
Sealed, unopened beverage bottles get allowed into some events with strict requirements that they remain sealed. Once opened, whether legally or by security inspection, the beverage must be consumed before entry.
Open containers—including partially consumed bottles—cannot enter any NRG event. This prevents spillage and potential contamination of event areas.
Beverage Alternatives
Purchasing beverages inside the stadium remains the simplest solution. Concession prices run $8-12 for bottled water, comparable to other venues but higher than retail. METRO stations and nearby parking areas offer quick convenience store access before arriving at the stadium.
Bringing empty reusable bottles to fill at water fountains provides a practical option when permitted. Ask at guest services upon arrival if refill stations are available.
Medical Necessity Bags
Medical equipment accommodations exist for genuine health needs. Insulin supply bags, EpiPen carriers, oxygen equipment cases, and similar medical necessities get approved after inspection by security personnel.
The approval process doesn’t happen instantly at the gate. Those with significant medical equipment should arrive early or contact NRG security beforehand for expedited screening.
Medical Equipment Approval
Conditions requiring injectable medications or portable devices qualify for exceptions. Those managing diabetes, severe allergies, asthma, or cardiac conditions typically receive approval for appropriately-sized medical bags.
Documentation or medical ID cards expedite the process, though not required. Security makes reasonable accommodations based on obvious medical need and size appropriateness.
Required Security Screening
All medical bags undergo inspection by security staff. The inspection process may involve opening medication containers or requesting information about the equipment’s purpose. This standard inspection protects all attendees.
Cooperation with security makes screening faster. Having medical supplies organized and accessible speeds up the process considerably compared to frantically searching a bag for insulin or an EpiPen.
Medication & Device Bags
Purpose-designed medical supply bags made from semi-transparent or clear materials help expedite approval. These compact organizers made specifically for medical needs typically receive immediate clearance.
Bringing medications or devices in everyday bags slows approval because security must carefully inspect contents to verify they’re genuinely medical supplies rather than prohibited items.
Planning Your NRG Stadium Visit
The clear bag policy streamlines security while maintaining atmosphere. Bringing a clear bag or approved clutch ensures smooth entry without frustration.
NRG Park’s official guidance remains the definitive source for all policy questions. Bookmark this resource and check event-specific details before attending special events like FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.
Contact NRG directly for unique situations involving medical equipment or special needs. Guest services can provide guidance on accommodations before event day.
Plan to arrive early on game day. Even with proper bags, peak entry times create lines. The clear bag policy accelerates screening, but volume still affects wait times during major events.
FAQs
Can I bring a backpack?
No, backpacks of any size are prohibited. Use clear bags or approved clutches only.
Will NRG store my prohibited bag?
No. NRG offers no storage facilities or bag holding services. Plan your approach accordingly.
Do clear bags need specific branding?
No. Generic clear freezer bags work perfectly. No special stadium purchase is required.
What if my bag is slightly over 12″ x 6″ x 12″?
Security will deny it. The dimensions have no tolerance for oversized bags.
Can children bring different bags?
No. The policy applies uniformly to all attendees regardless of age.
Are designer clear bags permitted?
Yes, if they meet size requirements and are genuinely transparent throughout.
Do the dimensions include handles?
No. Measurements exclude attached handles or straps and measure the main bag body only.
Can I attend FIFA World Cup 2026 with the same policy?
FIFA matches will maintain the NRG clear bag policy with potentially additional restrictions during tournament events. Check final FIFA guidance closer to match dates.
What happens if I arrive with a prohibited bag?
You must return to your vehicle or leave the bag behind. Security will not hold items or provide storage.
Are hydration packs allowed?
Hydration packs function as backpacks and are prohibited. Use approved clear bags instead.

title: Gillette Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: Gillette Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Gillette Stadium in Foxborough, Massachusetts, enforces a clear bag policy for all events, including FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.
Whether you’re attending a Patriots game, concert, or international soccer tournament, understanding the bag requirements prevents entry delays and rejections at security checkpoints.
This guide covers exactly what bags pass inspection, prohibited items, and how to prepare without hassle.
Quick Reference: Gillette Stadium Bag Policy Compliance Chart
|Bag Type||Size Limit||Material||Status||Notes|
|Clear backpack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Transparent frame required|
|Clear fanny pack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||One-gallon freezer bag alternative|
|Small clutch||4.5″ × 6.5″||Any (opaque OK)||✅ Allowed||Wallet-sized only|
|Diaper bag (clear)||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Subject to inspection|
|Standard backpack||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Even if under size limit|
|Opaque handbag||Over 4.5″ × 6.5″||Any||❌ Prohibited||Must go to storage|
|Rolling luggage||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Use external storage|
|Camera bag (non-clear)||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Only if transparent and compliant|
What Bags Are Allowed: The Clear Bag Policy at Gillette Stadium
Gillette Stadium enforces a clear bag policy consistent with NFL guidelines and FIFA security standards.
The policy is straightforward but specific. Understanding the exact dimensions and materials accepted prevents rejection at the gates. This policy applies to all events, from Patriots games to concerts and World Cup matches.
Clear Bag Requirements & Size Limits (12″ × 6″ × 12″)
Clear bags are the primary option for bringing items into Gillette Stadium. The maximum permitted size is 12 inches by 6 inches by 12 inches. In metric, that’s 30.5 × 15.25 × 30.5 centimeters.
This size accommodates clear backpacks, fanny packs, and cinch bags as long as they fit within these exact dimensions. Many bags marketed as “stadium-compliant” actually exceed this limit by a few inches. Measure before you go.
A one-gallon clear plastic freezer bag also qualifies if it stays completely transparent and doesn’t exceed the size threshold. These are cheap, accessible alternatives if you don’t own a compliant bag.
Small Clutches & Wristlets (6.5″ × 4.5″ Max)
Non-clear bags are permitted only if they stay under 6.5 inches by 4.5 inches. This is roughly palm-of-your-hand sizing. Think wallet-sized clutches, small crossbody bags, or minimalist pouches.
Handles and straps are acceptable. The overall dimensions are what matter. A crossbody clutch worn under your shirt works, though security may ask you to display it for verification.
One-Gallon Freezer Bags Permitted
A standard gallon-sized clear freezer bag meets Gillette’s policy requirements. These cost under $2 and offer a temporary solution if you don’t have a compliant bag. They’re genuinely see-through and fit the size specs.
Fill it with only essentials: ID, ticket, phone, wallet. Nothing more. Security inspects these just like any other bag.
What You Cannot Bring: Prohibited Items List at Gillette
Gillette Stadium maintains a comprehensive prohibited items list aligned with NFL and FIFA security standards.
According to NFL event safety guidelines, these restrictions exist to manage crowds, prevent security threats, and ensure evacuation safety. Gillette enforces this policy strictly across all 75,000 seats.
Backpacks, Suitcases & Travel Luggage Banned
Standard backpacks are prohibited regardless of size. This includes school backpacks, hiking packs, travel daypacks, and messenger bags with opaque frames.
The only backpack exception is a clear backpack meeting the 12×6×12 inch size limit with a fully transparent frame. Most backpacks have opaque padding or frames that automatically disqualify them.
Suitcases, rolling luggage, hard-sided cases, and overnight bags are all banned. These items block sightlines and create evacuation delays. If you’re traveling from out of town, check luggage at your hotel before arriving at the stadium.
Cameras & Recording Devices (Video Prohibited)
Personal phones are allowed. Professional cameras with detachable lenses longer than 6 inches are prohibited. This includes DSLRs, mirrorless cameras, and telephoto setups.
Tripods, monopods, and selfie sticks cannot enter. Drones and remote-controlled devices are banned. Professional audio recorders and Go-Pro cameras with mounting equipment are prohibited.
Weapons, Coolers & Food Restrictions
Weapons of any kind are banned. This includes firearms, ammunition, pellet guns, knives, box cutters, pepper spray, tear gas, mace, tasers, and stun guns.
Coolers, thermoses, and beverage containers are prohibited. You can purchase drinks inside the stadium. Flammable liquids, explosives, fireworks, and any suspicious devices are completely banned.
Special Policy Questions: Specific Items
Users often ask about items not explicitly mentioned in the main policy. Here are the specific answers.
Can You Bring a Water Bottle to Gillette Stadium?
Empty water bottles are generally allowed. You can bring an empty, reusable water bottle and fill it at water fountains inside the stadium. Some venues have water bottle refill stations.
Sealed bottles from outside may be confiscated. Check with Guest Services closer to your event for current policies on beverages.
Are Crossbody Bags Allowed at Gillette?
Crossbody bags are allowed if they meet the size requirements. If your crossbody bag is under 4.5×6.5 inches, it can be non-clear. If it’s larger, it must be clear and fit the 12×6×12 inch limit.
Test your bag dimensions before game day. Measure the main compartment, not including straps or handles. Many crossbody bags exceed limits without the owner realizing it.
Diaper Bag Exceptions for Parents
Parents can bring clear diaper bags that comply with the 12×6×12 inch policy. The bag must be transparent for inspection purposes.
Formula, baby food, medications, and inhalers are permitted after security inspects them. Medical equipment bags with oxygen, glucose monitors, or EpiPens receive similar treatment. Arrive early with documentation so staff can verify contents without delays.
For questions, contact Gillette Stadium Guest Services at (508) 543-1776.
Variations by Event Type: Event-Specific Rules at Gillette
Gillette Stadium hosts multiple event types, and some have slightly different rules depending on the event. Clear bag policy requirements remain consistent, but timing and crowd expectations vary by event type.
Gillette Patriots Game Bag Policy
NFL game days follow the standard clear bag policy at Gillette. The 12×6×12 inch clear bag limit applies. Small clutches under 4.5×6.5 inches are permitted.
Alcohol cannot be brought into Gillette Stadium. Beer and other beverages must be purchased inside. Arrive 90 minutes before kickoff to account for security screening during Patriots home games.
Bag Rules for Concerts & Events
Concert and entertainment events sometimes have modified bag policies at Gillette. Some artists or promoters may enforce stricter restrictions. Check the specific event’s website before arrival.
In general, assume the standard Gillette policy applies unless announced otherwise.
FIFA World Cup 2026 Enhanced Security
FIFA World Cup 2026 matches at Gillette will implement enhanced international security protocols. Expect stricter enforcement and longer screening times compared to regular season events.
The clear bag policy will remain the same, but staff will be more thorough with inspections. Arrive at least two to three hours before kickoff for World Cup matches. International visitor volumes will be significantly higher than typical events.
How Strictly Is the Policy Enforced?
The policy is enforced consistently. Knowing what happens if your bag doesn’t comply helps you plan accordingly.
Stadium Security Inspections & Procedures
Security personnel visually inspect clear bags without opening them when possible. Metal detectors scan for prohibited items. Pat-downs confirm compliance with policy.
Most fans with compliant bags clear security in under 30 seconds. Non-compliant bags trigger extended inspections, manual opening, and potential denial of entry. The process is faster if you arrive early and have a bag that clearly meets the size and transparency requirements.
Mobile Locker Options for Non-Compliant Bags
If your bag doesn’t meet the policy, external storage options exist. The Mobile Locker Company operates secure lockers outside the stadium.
Pricing: $10-$15 per bag (varies by event). You check in your non-compliant bag, receive a digital claim ID on your phone, and collect it after the event. Check-in takes about 90 seconds. Email them at hello@themobilelockerco.com for pricing on your specific event date.
Locker availability runs from 2 hours before event start until 1.5 hours after the event concludes. Items left overnight are held securely. Contact The Mobile Locker Company if you need to retrieve items from previous events. Gates 3 and 4 have convenient locker access.
What Happens if You Violate the Policy?
Security will deny entry with a non-compliant bag. You have two options: use the Mobile Locker Company or go without the bag.
Attempting to sneak prohibited items into the stadium results in ejection and possible arrest. The policy is non-negotiable for safety and security reasons. Don’t test it.
Additional Stadium Information
Getting to Gillette Stadium and understanding the broader logistics matters for your visit.
Parking at Gillette Stadium
Gillette offers extensive parking with over 20,000 spaces. Parking costs vary by event, typically $15-$30. Arrive early for premium parking locations closer to the stadium.
If you’re taking public transportation, MBTA commuter rail serves the area. The Providence/Stoughton line has a station near Gillette. Rideshare services are also available from nearby towns.
Mobile Ticketing Requirements
Mobile tickets are standard at Gillette. Download the official NFL or event app and have your ticket ready on your phone. Physical tickets are rarely accepted.
Arrive with your phone fully charged. Have your ticket visible at entry gates. Mobile ticketing speeds up the entry process compared to searching for physical tickets.
Getting to Foxborough from Boston
Foxborough is about 35 miles southwest of Boston. Driving takes 45 minutes to an hour depending on traffic. Take I-95 South to Route 1 South, then follow stadium signs.
From Boston’s South Station, the MBTA commuter rail reaches Gillette’s area. The trip takes about 60 minutes. Plan extra time on game days when public transit is crowded. Rideshare from Boston costs $30-$50 depending on demand.
FAQs
Is there a clear bag policy at Gillette Stadium for FIFA World Cup matches?
Yes. All bags must either be clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC measuring no more than 12×6×12 inches, or non-clear bags under 4.5×6.5 inches. Gillette Stadium’s official policy requires transparent visibility of contents for both regular season and World Cup 2026 matches.
Can I bring a standard backpack into Gillette Stadium?
No. Standard backpacks are prohibited regardless of size. Clear backpacks meeting the 12×6×12 inch limit with fully transparent frames are the only exception. Opaque backpacks will be denied at entry.
What happens if my bag doesn’t comply with Gillette’s policy?
Security will turn you away. The Mobile Locker Company operates storage outside the stadium for $10-$15 per bag. You can retrieve it after the event. Contact them at hello@themobilelockerco.com for pricing details.
Are diaper bags allowed at Gillette Stadium?
Yes. Diaper bags must be clear and meet the 12×6×12 inch size requirement. Formula, baby food, medications, and medical supplies are permitted after inspection.
What’s the maximum size for a non-clear bag or clutch at Gillette?
Non-clear bags are allowed only if under 4.5×6.5 inches, roughly palm-sized. This includes wallet clutches and small crossbody bags. Anything larger must be clear and meet the 12×6×12 inch limit.
Can I bring a camera or personal phone to Gillette Stadium?
Personal phones are allowed. Professional cameras with detachable lenses longer than 6 inches are prohibited. Tripods, recording devices, and mounting equipment are banned. Check the official guidelines for questions about specific equipment or call Guest Services at (508) 543-1776.

title: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
Atlanta is hosting FIFA World Cup 2026, and Mercedes-Benz Stadium will draw fans from around the globe.
Before you arrive at the gates, understand the clear bag policy that governs entry. This isn’t just a stadium preference. It’s a safety standard that FIFA enforces across all tournament venues to manage security efficiently and protect the international crowd.
Knowing what bags pass inspection means avoiding entry delays, missed moments, and the frustration of turning back after traveling to the stadium. Whether you’re a first-time visitor or seasoned supporter, this guide answers every question about what you can bring inside.
Quick Reference: Mercedes-Benz Stadium Bag Policy Compliance Chart
|Bag Type||Size Limit||Material||Status||Notes|
|Clear backpack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Transparent frame required|
|Clear fanny pack||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||One-gallon freezer bag alternative|
|Small clutch||4.5″ × 6.5″||Any (opaque OK)||✅ Allowed||Wallet-sized only|
|Diaper bag (clear)||12″ × 6″ × 12″||Plastic, vinyl, PVC||✅ Allowed||Subject to inspection|
|Standard backpack||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Even if under size limit|
|Opaque handbag||Over 4.5″ × 6.5″||Any||❌ Prohibited||Must go to storage|
|Rolling luggage||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Use Mobile Locker Company|
|Camera bag (non-clear)||Any||Any||❌ Prohibited||Only if transparent and compliant|
Why Clear Bags Are Required for FIFA Security
Clear bags aren’t an inconvenience.
They’re a deliberate safety mechanism that FIFA mandates at all World Cup venues. Unlike typical NFL events at the same stadium, FIFA tournaments require elevated security due to international attendance and the global visibility of the event.
Transparent visibility allows security personnel to inspect bag contents without opening them, which accelerates screening and reduces bottlenecks at entry gates during peak arrival times.
FIFA vs. NFL Security Standards
The difference matters because FIFA operates under distinct international security protocols.
While the same stadium hosts both NFL and soccer events, FIFA implements stricter standards aligned with global safety requirements. Transparent bags let security confirm what’s inside in seconds rather than minutes.
This distinction isn’t just procedural. It reflects how FIFA coordinates security across three countries (USA, Canada, Mexico) and ensures consistent screening regardless of a visitor’s home country or language.
How Clear Bags Accelerate Entry
Security personnel can visually confirm bag contents without hand searches. Metal detectors scan for prohibited items, and pat-downs confirm compliance.
Most fans clear screening in 30 seconds with a compliant bag. Non-compliant bags trigger extended inspections, manual opening, and potential denial.
The sports planning guide for World Cup 2026 emphasizes arriving 90 minutes early to account for screening lines during high-volume events like quarter-final matches.
Airport-Style Screening Explained
Think of stadium security like airport TSA checkpoints. You place your bag on a conveyor belt leading through a metal detector. Staff visually inspect the clear interior. If anything looks questionable, you’ll be asked to explain or remove the item.
This system works only if bags are actually transparent. Bring hand-sized essentials: ID, ticket, phone, wallet. The faster you move through, the more time you have to enjoy the fan experience before kickoff.
Allowed Bag Sizes and Materials
The clear bag policy has two tiers: primary and secondary. Primary bags (clear, large) must meet strict dimensions. Secondary bags (small, non-clear) are allowed only if they stay within clutch size limits.
Clear Bags: 12×6×12 Inches
This is the maximum permitted size for clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC bags.
In centimeters, that’s 30.5 × 15.25 × 30.5 cm.
The specification includes clear backpacks, fanny packs, and cinch bags as long as they fit within these dimensions. Many bags marketed as “stadium-compliant” exceed this limit by a few inches.
Measure yours before game day.
A gallon-sized freezer bag also qualifies if completely clear and under the size threshold.
Small Clutches: 4.5×6.5 Inches
Non-clear bags are allowed only if they don’t exceed this tiny footprint.
This means wallet-sized clutches, small crossbody bags, or minimalist pouches. Think of it as palm-of-your-hand sizing.
Handles and straps are fine as long as the overall dimensions stay under 4.5×6.5 inches. A crossbody clutch worn under your shirt works, though security may ask you to show it’s compliant.
Approved Materials (Plastic, Vinyl, PVC)
Only transparent, flexible materials pass inspection.
Tinted, frosted, or patterned bags are not allowed even if they’re technically see-through. Simple logos on one side are acceptable. Complex graphics that obstruct interior visibility will be denied.
Avoid bags with metallic hardware, rigid frames, or decorative elements that block the view inside. If you can’t see all interior contents from every angle, it fails.
Approved bag materials:
- Clear plastic (most common)
- Vinyl (flexible, durable)
- PVC (rigid clarity, airport-style)
- One-gallon freezer bags (temporary option)
- Transparent backpacks (if under size limit)
Specific Bag Types and Exceptions
Some bags need special consideration, and certain populations have legitimate exceptions to standard rules.
Diaper Bags and Medical Bags
Parents traveling with infants and toddlers can bring clear diaper bags that comply with size policy.
The bag must be clear and meet the 12×6×12 inch limit.
Security will inspect diaper bags just like any other. Formula, baby food, medications, and inhalers are exempted after proper inspection.
Medical equipment bags (oxygen, glucose monitors, EpiPens) receive similar treatment. Arrive early with documentation if you’re carrying specialized medical gear so staff can verify contents without delays.
Non-Clear Bags Rules
The only way to bring an opaque bag is if it stays under 4.5×6.5 inches.
Larger opaque bags mean entry denied, no exceptions.
Some fans ask if they can bring a non-clear bag in addition to a clear bag. The answer is yes, but the non-clear bag must be small enough to fit inside or carry separately while still complying with size limits. You can’t use a large opaque bag as your primary carrier.
Transparency and Hardware Requirements
Every angle of your bag’s interior must be visible without opening it.
Buckles, grommets, or metal décor that conceals interior space will get your bag rejected at the gate. Simpler designs pass faster.
If your bag has opaque side pockets or hidden compartments, it won’t meet the visual inspection standard. Test your bag at home by looking through it from all sides. If you can’t see the entire interior from the outside, security won’t let it through.
Prohibited Items and Restrictions
Some bags are outright banned, and certain items can’t be brought inside regardless of bag type.
Backpacks Not Allowed
Standard backpacks are prohibited.
This applies to school backpacks, hiking packs, and travel daypacks. The one exception: clear backpacks that don’t exceed 12×6×12 inches.
The frame and straps must also be transparent or at least not obstruct the view inside. Most backpacks, even small ones, have opaque frames or padding that makes them non-compliant.
Large Luggage Restrictions
Suitcases, rolling luggage, hard-sided cases, and overnight bags are all banned.
These items create evacuation delays and block sightlines. If you’re traveling from out of town, check your luggage at your hotel or use external storage services before heading to the stadium.
This rule applies regardless of whether luggage is locked or empty.
Item-Specific Bans
Weapons and safety items:
- Explosives, fireworks, flares
- Firearms, ammunition, pellet guns
- Knives, box cutters, sharp objects
- Pepper spray, tear gas, mace
- Tasers, stun guns, cattle prods
Recording and professional equipment:
- Cameras with lenses longer than 6 inches
- Tripods, monopods, selfie sticks
- Drones and remote-controlled devices
- Professional audio recorders
- Go-Pro cameras and mounting equipment
Prohibited substances:
- Alcohol (beer, wine, spirits)
- Illegal drugs and controlled substances
- Loose batteries outside working devices
- Flammable liquids, lighter fluid
- Any explosive or dangerous materials
Other restricted items:
- Noisemakers (vuvuzelas, air horns, whistles)
- Umbrellas of any kind
- Portable chairs, stools, booster seats
- Selfie sticks and pole-mounted items
- Flags larger than 1.5 meters
- Masks that conceal identity
- Tobacco and vaping products
- Glass bottles, aluminum cans
- Any item security deems suspicious
What to Do If Your Bag Doesn’t Comply
You show up with a non-compliant bag.
Security turns you away. You have options.
External Bag Storage Options
The Mobile Locker Company operates fee-based storage just outside the stadium gates.
Their secure lockers accept bags that don’t meet the clear policy. You check in your bag, receive a digital claim ID on your phone, and collect it after the match.
No pre-booking required. First-come, first-serve.
Lockers are available from 2 hours before kickoff until 1.5 hours after the event ends. Contact The Mobile Locker Company for pricing and specific gate locations.
Mobile Locker Company Details
Locations are positioned near Gates 3 and 4 for convenient access before you hit the main security checkpoint.
Digital check-in takes 90 seconds. Staff answers questions on-site. Items left overnight are held, and retrieval is handled via email.
For most matches, this solution costs $10-$15 per bag depending on event size.
Planning Your Stadium Arrival
The stadium’s recommendation is blunt: don’t bring a bag.
It’s the fastest entry path.
If you must bring essentials, pack only ID, ticket, phone, and wallet in a compliant clear bag or small clutch.
Arrive 90 minutes before kickoff. Plan for 20 to 40 minutes of security screening during high-attendance matches like group-stage finals.
Use public transit via MARTA. Take the Blue/Green Line to GWCC/Vine City station (2-block walk to stadium). Or use rideshare to avoid parking delays and stress.
Before You Head to the Stadium
Plan your bag strategy early.
The simplest approach: skip bags entirely. You’ll clear security fastest with just ID, ticket, and phone.
If you prefer to carry essentials, invest in a clear plastic bag under 12×6×12 inches from any retailer. They cost $5-$15.
Measure your bag.
Don’t estimate. Use a measuring tape on width, depth, and height. One inch over the limit means denial.
Test transparency by looking through it from every angle. If you can’t see the entire interior, it won’t pass inspection.
Arrive 90 minutes early.
Security screening during high-attendance matches takes 20 to 40 minutes. You’re competing with thousands of international fans all entering simultaneously.
Early arrival gives you buffer time, access to bag storage if needed, and time to explore the stadium concourse before kickoff.
Know your prohibited items.
Weapons, explosives, large cameras, umbrellas, noisemakers, alcohol, and flammable materials are banned.
These rules apply to everyone, no exceptions.
If your bag doesn’t comply, use Mobile Locker Company.
They operate outside Gates 3 and 4 with secure digital check-in. Cost is typically $10-$15 per event.
Retrieve your bag up to 1.5 hours after the match concludes.
FAQs
Is there a clear bag policy at Mercedes-Benz Stadium for FIFA matches?
Yes. All bags entering the stadium must either be clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC measuring no more than 12×6×12 inches, or non-clear bags under 4.5×6.5 inches. FIFA security protocols require transparent visibility of bag contents.
Can I bring a backpack into the stadium?
No, standard backpacks are prohibited. Clear backpacks that meet the 12×6×12 inch size limit and have transparent frames are the only exception. Opaque backpacks of any size will be denied entry.
What if I bring a bag that doesn’t comply?
Security will turn you away at the gate. The Mobile Locker Company provides fee-based external storage outside the stadium. You can store non-compliant bags and retrieve them after the match.
Are diaper bags allowed?
Yes, diaper bags must be clear and comply with the 12×6×12 inch size policy. Formula, medications, and baby supplies are permitted after security inspection. Arrive early to allow time for additional screening.
What size is a small, non-clear clutch?
Non-clear bags are allowed only if they don’t exceed 4.5×6.5 inches, about palm-of-your-hand size. This qualifies as a wallet-sized clutch or small crossbody bag. Anything larger must be clear.
Can I bring a camera or phone to record?
Personal phones are allowed. Professional cameras with detachable lenses longer than 6 inches are prohibited. Tripods and recording devices are banned. Check stadium policy if you have questions about specific equipment.

title: Lincoln Financial Field Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026 - surprisesports
snippet: Lincoln Financial Field Bag Policy for FIFA World Cup 2026  surprisesports
content:
You’re packing your bag the morning of a World Cup match and you’re not sure if what you’ve got will get you through the gate. That’s a stressful thing to figure out on the day.
The short answer: a clear plastic bag up to 12″ x 6″ x 12″ gets you in without any issues. So does a one-gallon Ziploc or a small clutch bag. Anything else and you’re risking a trip back to the car.
One thing to know before you go: FIFA renames host venues for the tournament. During the 6 World Cup matches, Lincoln Financial Field officially becomes “Philadelphia Stadium” on all FIFA apps, tickets, and signage.
It’s the same building. If your ticket or the FIFA app shows Philadelphia Stadium, that’s where you’re going.
This guide covers what bags are allowed, what gets you turned away at the gate, what’s different about World Cup security compared to an Eagles game, and what international fans need to know before arriving.
Bags That Are Allowed at Lincoln Financial Field for FIFA World Cup 2026
Here’s exactly what you can bring in:
|Bag Type||Max Size||Clear Required?||Notes|
|Clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC bag||12″ x 6″ x 12″||Yes||Most common compliant option|
|One-gallon Ziploc bag||Standard gallon size||Yes (transparent by nature)||Simplest option if you don’t have a stadium bag|
|Small clutch bag||4.5″ x 6.5″||No||Permitted in addition to your clear bag, not instead of it|
|Empty refillable water bottle||Any size||No||Confirmed permitted for FIFA matches by the City of Philadelphia’s official guide|
A few things worth clarifying from that table.
The small clutch bag (4.5″ x 6.5″) is something a lot of people misread. You can bring it alongside your clear bag, not as a replacement for one.
If your only bag is a small clutch, you’re fine for what fits inside it. But if you need to carry more, you still need a clear bag too.
The empty refillable water bottle is specifically confirmed for FIFA Club World Cup 2025 matches at this venue by the City of Philadelphia’s official safety guide. This is a FIFA-layer permission that doesn’t appear in the standard Eagles bag policy.
Bring one, especially in summer. Philadelphia in late June can hit 30°C, and water stations are located at sections 103, 118, 122, C3, C19, C24, C38, 204, and 222 throughout the stadium.
Bags Not Allowed (What Gets You Turned Away at the Gate)
If you show up with any of these, you’re not getting in. There’s no bag check or storage on site, so it means a trip back to your car or leaving it behind entirely.
- Backpacks of any size, including drawstring bags, cinch bags, and hydration packs
- Fanny packs and hip bags
- Camera bags and cases
- Briefcases and laptop bags
- Oversized purses larger than 4.5″ x 6.5″
- Coolers of any type
- Duffel bags and gym bags
- Luggage and travel bags
- Grocery bags and tote bags
The most common mistake is the backpack. A lot of fans assume a small or packable daypack is fine. It’s not. The clear bag policy applies universally, and backpacks are excluded regardless of size or material.
If you arrive with a prohibited bag and there’s no car to run it back to, your only option is third-party luggage storage near the stadium. Stasher and Bounce both have locations in central Philadelphia. Book ahead on match days since they fill up fast.
FIFA Rules That Don’t Apply to Eagles Games
This is the section most people don’t know about, and it catches fans off guard at the gate.
The standard NFL clear bag policy forms the base layer at Lincoln Financial Field. But FIFA adds its own rules on top of that for FIFA World Cup 2026 matches. These rules don’t exist for Eagles games, so even longtime season ticket holders need to read this.
Pyrotechnics and smoke bombs are explicitly banned.
This is a FIFA-specific rule, not an NFL one. Smoke bombs and flares have become increasingly common in soccer fan culture globally. At World Cup venues across the US, they’re prohibited entirely.
If you’re coming from a supporter culture where this is part of the matchday experience, leave them at home. Security will confiscate them and you could be removed from the stadium.
Vuvuzelas and noisemakers are not permitted inside the stadium.
This one surprises a lot of fans arriving from South Africa, Latin America, and Eastern Europe where vuvuzelas are standard matchday kit.
FIFA banned them from stadiums following widespread complaints after the 2010 World Cup. They’re not allowed at any FIFA World Cup 2026 venue in the US, including Lincoln Financial Field.
The venue name changes for World Cup matches.
FIFA’s corporate naming policy requires stripping commercial names from all host venues during the tournament. Lincoln Financial Field becomes “Philadelphia Stadium” for all 6 matches.
If you’re navigating via the FIFA app, booking transport through a local service, or sharing directions with someone, the name you’ll see is Philadelphia Stadium. It’s the same building at the same address. Just be aware so it doesn’t cause confusion on the day.
What Security Looks Like on Match Day
If your last experience at Lincoln Financial Field was an Eagles game, World Cup security will feel noticeably different.
FIFA World Cup security inspection is closer to what you’d encounter at an airport than at a regular NFL game. Confirmed for the FIFA Club World Cup 2025 matches at this venue: expect pat-downs at entry, expanded screening lanes, and walk-through weapons detection at all gates.
This isn’t a response to any specific threat. It’s the standard security protocol FIFA implements at all World Cup venues in partnership with the Department of Homeland Security.
For international fans who’ve attended club football or international matches in Europe, South America, or Africa, this level of security will probably feel more familiar than it does to Americans used to NFL-standard entry.
US stadium security has historically been less intensive than major tournament security elsewhere.
Arrive at least 90 minutes before kickoff. That’s the guidance FIFA issued during the Club World Cup 2025 matches at this venue, and it held up practically.
The expanded screening takes more time than a typical Eagles entry, and lines build quickly once the 60-minute mark passes.
Medical, Diaper Bag, and Camera Exceptions
Medical Items
Insulin, portable oxygen, breast pumps, and other medical necessities are permitted. Don’t just show up and hope the gate staff waves you through.
Contact Fan Services before match day at 215-463-5500 or contact@lincolnfinancialfield.com and arrange medical exception entry in advance.
When you arrive, use the North Gate, which is located next to the flagpoles by the Xfinity Gate. That’s the designated entry point for all medical exceptions.
Diaper Bags
Diaper bags are permitted. If you’ve seen older PDFs floating around that say otherwise, those are out of date.
The current live FAQ (updated December 2025) confirms diaper bags are allowed. Use a clear diaper bag if possible since it speeds up your entry at security.
Cameras
Camera lenses must be 5 inches or shorter. You’ll find several sources online saying 6 inches. That’s incorrect. The limit at Lincoln Financial Field is 5 inches, and security measures on-site. Don’t pack a long telephoto lens and assume you’ll talk your way in.
Is There a Bag Check at Lincoln Financial Field?
No. There is no on-site bag storage or bag check at Lincoln Financial Field for FIFA World Cup 2026 matches.
If you arrive with a prohibited bag, your options are:
- Return it to your car
- Use a third-party luggage storage service nearby
The closest options are Stasher and Bounce, both with locations in central Philadelphia. On match days these fill up quickly, so if you think there’s any chance you’ll need bag storage, book a slot in advance rather than searching on the day.
What International Fans Need to Know Before Arriving
Clear bag rules don’t exist at football stadiums in most of the world. If you’re traveling to Philadelphia from Europe, South America, Africa, or elsewhere, this policy is going to feel unusual. Here’s why it exists.
The NFL introduced the clear bag policy across all member stadiums after 2013 as part of a security partnership with the Department of Homeland Security.
The logic is straightforward: a clear bag lets security staff visually check contents quickly without opening every single bag in a long queue. FIFA adopted the same standard for US host venues for the 2026 tournament.
You’re not being singled out. Every fan at every FIFA World Cup 2026 venue in the US is subject to the same policy, including local fans who’ve been attending games at these stadiums for years.
A few other things worth knowing:
The FIFA Fan Fest is at Lemon Hill in Fairmount Park, not at the stadium. It’s a separate venue with its own entry rules. Check the Fan Fest entry requirements independently before you go, as they may differ from the stadium policy.
Carry your passport or travel document separately from your bag if possible. FIFA World Cup 2026 uses a hybrid ticketing system and some gates may require identity verification alongside your ticket.
SEPTA’s Broad Street Line runs directly to the stadium. During match days, service is increased and it’s genuinely faster than driving and parking. From Center City, it’s about 15 minutes to the station nearest the stadium at Pattison/AT&T Station.
FAQs
What bags are allowed at FIFA World Cup 2026 stadiums?
Clear plastic, vinyl, or PVC bags up to 12″ x 6″ x 12″, one-gallon Ziploc bags, and small clutch bags up to 4.5″ x 6.5″. Empty refillable water bottles are also permitted at Lincoln Financial Field.
Can you bring a backpack to Lincoln Financial Field for World Cup 2026?
No. Backpacks of all sizes are prohibited under the clear bag policy, including small daypacks, drawstring bags, and cinch bags.
Is the bag policy the same for FIFA games as Eagles games?
The base clear bag rules are the same. But FIFA adds extra restrictions on top, including explicit bans on pyrotechnics, smoke bombs, and vuvuzelas that don’t appear in the standard Eagles policy.
What is NOT allowed at FIFA World Cup 2026 at Lincoln Financial Field?
Backpacks, fanny packs, camera bags, briefcases, oversized purses, coolers, duffel bags, luggage, grocery bags, pyrotechnics, smoke bombs, and vuvuzelas.
Can I bring a vuvuzela to FIFA World Cup 2026?
No. Vuvuzelas and noisemakers are not permitted inside the stadium at any FIFA World Cup 2026 venue in the US.
Is there a bag check at Lincoln Financial Field for World Cup matches?
No. There’s no on-site bag storage. If you arrive with a prohibited bag, you’ll need to return it to your car or use a third-party storage service in the city.
What is “Philadelphia Stadium” — is it the same as Lincoln Financial Field?
Yes, same building. FIFA’s corporate naming policy strips commercial names from all host venues during the tournament. Lincoln Financial Field is called “Philadelphia Stadium” on all FIFA platforms, apps, and signage during the 6 World Cup matches.
Can I bring food or drinks into Lincoln Financial Field for FIFA World Cup?
Outside food and drinks are generally not permitted. Empty refillable water bottles are allowed and you can fill them at water stations throughout the stadium.
How strict is security at FIFA World Cup 2026 USA stadiums?
Stricter than a typical NFL game. Expect airport-style screening with pat-downs and walk-through weapons detection. Arrive at least 90 minutes before kickoff.
Are clear bags required at all FIFA World Cup 2026 venues in the US? Yes.
All 11 FIFA World Cup 2026 host venues in the US have adopted the clear bag policy.
Can I bring a camera to FIFA World Cup 2026 at Lincoln Financial Field?
Yes, but the lens must be 5 inches or shorter. Several sources incorrectly say 6 inches. The limit at Lincoln Financial Field is 5 inches.
What should I do if I have a medical item that doesn’t fit in a clear bag?
Contact Fan Services before match day at 215-463-5500 or contact@lincolnfinancialfield.com. Arrange medical exception entry in advance and use the North Gate (next to flagpoles by the Xfinity Gate) when you arrive.
Can I bring an empty water bottle to Lincoln Financial Field for FIFA World Cup?
Yes. Empty refillable water bottles are confirmed permitted for FIFA matches at Lincoln Financial Field, per the City of Philadelphia’s official FIFA Club World Cup safety guide.
What time should I arrive at Lincoln Financial Field for a FIFA World Cup match?
At least 90 minutes before kickoff. FIFA World Cup security screening takes longer than a standard Eagles game, and lines build quickly within the final hour.
Is the FIFA Fan Fest at Lemon Hill subject to the same bag policy?
The Fan Fest at Lemon Hill is a separate venue with its own entry rules. Check the FIFA Fan Fest entry requirements independently before attending, as they may differ from the stadium policy.

title: Dallas’ FIFA World Cup 2026 Tech Roundtable Takes the Long View, With a Smart City Focus - Dallas Innovates
snippet: Dallas’ FIFA World Cup 2026 Tech Roundtable Takes the Long View, With a Smart City Focus  Dallas Innovates
content:
Jennifer Sanders, executive director of the North Texas Innovation Alliance, says technology and infrastructure investments made for the FIFA World Cup 2026 can be leveraged to improve the quality of life of North Texas residents long after the event is over.
Earlier this month, the nonprofit group co-hosted a closed technology roundtable with the City of Fort Worth that focused on innovation initiatives around the World Cup. Dallas last year was named a host city for the event—a professional soccer tournament held every four years featuring national soccer teams—for the first time since 1994. And NTXIA, which is a consortium of municipalities, government agencies, tech companies, and academic institutions working to implement a smart region strategy for North Texas, is playing a key role in local preparations for the tournament.
“An event the magnitude of the World Cup presents opportunities to zoom out, get creative, and think big,” Sanders says. “Seizing and shepherding those opportunities is the key.”
Sanders, who also co-founded NTXIA, says the goal of her group is to “thread ‘big idea’ innovation projects throughout World Cup planning” that will have long-range impacts. Those impacts could range from parking and airport logistics to public safety, sustainability, and cybersecurity.
“The implementation of smart city infrastructure and a collaborative tech consortium between cities and municipalities ramping up for the World Cup will have transformative long-term benefits for the region,” Sanders says. “By establishing such innovations now, the region can develop a solid foundation for sustainable urban development and improved quality of life.
“The smart city infrastructure, including sensors, data analytics and connectivity, can be repurposed after the event to address various city challenges like traffic management, energy efficiency, waste management, and public transportation. These are projects that cities have dreamed of implementing that could be realized through the proving ground that this event provides.”
In the realm of public safety, Sanders says innovations and new smart technology could lead to faster response times in case of an emergency at the World Cup.
“For example, using a network of sensors and cameras, authorities can detect crowd density and manage crowd control measures to prevent overcrowding and aid traffic flow, particularly in travel between accommodations, events, and fan-fests occurring across the region,” she says. “Additionally, advanced analytics and predictive models can help anticipate potential security threats, enabling proactive security measures to be deployed effectively.
“Emerging solutions that can identify and facilitate interventions to human trafficking is a critical threat that we also believe technology can support.”
Challenges and opportunities discussed
At the tech roundtable on June 12, NTXIA and the City of Fort Worth hosted representatives from the Dallas Sports Commission and the Fort Worth Sports Commission, as well as representatives from municipalities, regional planning groups, economic development organizations, nonprofits, and the private sector.
While the initial discussion focused on broad challenges and opportunities connected with the 2026 event, future meetings will hone in on the participants’ individual priorities and perspectives.
Sanders says people and organizations interested in inquiring about future World Cup roundtables should contact NTXIA at [email protected].
Dallas is one of the 2026 World Cup’s 16 host cities in three countries, along with Atlanta, Boston, Guadalajara, Houston, Kansas City, Los Angeles, Mexico City, Miami, Monterrey, New York/New Jersey, Philadelphia, San Francisco, Seattle, Toronto, and Vancouver.
NTXIA will be working with these host cities as well as learning from other cities that previously hosted the event, Sanders says.
AT&T Stadium in Arlington will be one of the main venues for the 2026 tournament. The venue for the big final match hasn’t been announced yet.
